title: senecio panduriformis
intro: Senecio panduriformis, also known as bushy groundsel or prickly groundsel, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Senecio panduriformis include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to aid in the healing of wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Senecio panduriformis is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the need for proper dosing.
